title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
description: Narrated by Death, this poignant story follows a young girl named Liesel living in Nazi Germany, who finds solace and survival by stealing books and sharing them with her foster family and the Jewish man hidden in her basement.

title: Echoes of the Forgotten
author: Michael Nunn
description: On the first night of their third year of university, five students disappear without a trace. Three days later, Will Campbell is found miles away, in the middle of the countryside, covered in somebody else’s blood. His friends are nowhere to be found. And Will can’t remember anything except a fairground and a haunting man with a crooked smile.

title: Who Moved My Cheese?
author: Spencer Johnson
description: An influential business fable featuring four characters trapped in a maze who react differently to sudden changes in their environment, offering valuable life lessons on adapting quickly to disruption.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: An allegorical novel following a young Andalusian shepherd named Santiago on his journey to Egypt in search of a worldly treasure, discovering the true meaning of his 'Personal Legend' along the way.

title: The Quantum Horizon
author: Dr. Sarah Jenkins
description: An accessible exploration of quantum mechanics, entanglement, and how the subatomic world shapes our macro reality.

title: Before the Coffee Gets Cold
author: Toshikazu Kawaguchi
description: A comforting yet melancholic tale set in a small Tokyo café where customers are given the unique chance to travel back in time, provided they follow strict rules and return before their coffee gets cold.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A timeless masterpiece that delves into the decadence and disillusionment of the Jazz Age, exploring themes of wealth, love, and the American Dream in the Roaring Twenties.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A profound exploration of racial injustice and the destruction of innocence in the American South, seen through the eyes of young Scout Finch.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling dystopian masterpiece exploring the psychological terrors of life under Big Brother, totalitarian surveillance, and the erasure of individual free thought.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: The classic coming-of-age story tracking the disillusioned teenager Holden Caulfield as he wanders New York City examining the 'phoniness' of the adult world.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A romantic classic exploring the turbulent relationship between Elizabeth Bennet and Fitzwilliam Darcy, masterfully critiquing class and societal expectations.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: An epic adventure following the reluctant Bilbo Baggins as he leaves his peaceful home to help a group of dwarves reclaim their stolen treasure from a dragon.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The magical story of an orphaned boy who discovers his wizarding heritage and attends Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: An allegorical novel following an Andalusian shepherd named Santiago in search of a worldly treasure, discovering the true meaning of his 'Personal Legend'.

title: The Girl on the Train
author: Paula Hawkins
description: A psychological thriller about an alcoholic commuter who becomes entangled in a missing persons investigation after witnessing something shocking from her train window.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
description: A timeless masterpiece of strategy and insight, The Art of War by Sun Tzu is one of the most influential works ever written on leadership, warfare, and the psychology of conflict. Composed in ancient China around the 5th century BC, this legendary treatise goes beyond military tactics; it teaches how to lead, think, adapt, and triumph in any arena of life.
